threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’re still wearing shoes on the Clissold Park grass; everyone else is barefoot and getting sunburned.
Last Saturday on the 3 bus, someone tried to start a poetry slam between stops. Only half-listened, but it somehow felt like a Brixton thing to do.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week I saw a guy painting a mural while his dog sat beside him with a tiny beret. Only in Neukölln would art and canine fashion clash so beautifully.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you casually bump into a techno party in an old abandoned warehouse on a Monday morning. It’s both surreal and spectacular.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they know to avoid BART delays and take the 14 instead, even when it's the 'most delayed' bus; they’ve got the rhythm down.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a passenger in the back of a crowded bus serenading everyone with an off-key rendition of 'I Left My Heart in San Francisco.' Unexpectedly heartwarming.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can walk into a bar and get served immediately, but locals know the real vibe is to grab a drink and chat with friends while waiting.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they still look for the nearest metro sign instead of hopping on a bus; locals have memorized the routes.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' and look absolutely lost trying to navigate the rail map.
Last night on the 333, a guy serenaded us with an accordion. The driver just shook his head while everyone recorded it. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a toddler asking for a bite of a doner kebab from the vendor like it was the most natural thing ever. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the street art in Friedrichshain changes with the seasons? Summer murals turn to winter warnings as if the walls are alive.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their disdain for the BART delays; they know every workaround and have a backup taco stand in mind.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, a tourist asked a Muni driver for directions to the Painted Ladies, and the driver just laughed and said, 'Just stay on board, you'll get there eventually.'

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best tapas are on Carrer de Verdi, but the hidden gems are on the side streets. You're missing out on the real flavors.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their hesitation at crossing the Plaça del Sol—the locals zip through traffic like it’s a game, while the fresh faces look like deer in headlights.
